title: Revision history UI (view and restore previous snapshot)
description: Build a per-page revision history screen listing past revisions (author, timestamp, message), allowing an editor to preview a past snapshot's content and restore it.
acceptanceCriteria: `/admin/pages/:pageId/history` route lists revisions newest-first, showing author email, timestamp, and message (if any)
Selecting a revision shows a read-only preview of that snapshot's content (all tabs)
A 'Restaurar' action on a past revision calls the restore endpoint (TASK-36) and confirms via a dialog before applying, since restoring overwrites current draft/live blocks
After restoring, the admin panel navigates back to the page's content editor showing the restored content

steps.details: Add `revisions: { listByPage: protectedProcedure.input(z.object({ pageId: z.string() })).query(...) }` to a new `apps/server/src/trpc/routers/revisions.ts`, joining `users` for author email, ordered by `criado_em` descending.
Create `apps/admin/src/features/revisions/RevisionHistoryList.tsx` rendering the list with author, relative timestamp, and message.

On selecting a revision, fetch its full `snapshot_json` (add `revisions.getById` query) and render it read-only using `blocksToTiptapDoc` (TASK-31) per tab, in a non-editable Tiptap instance or a simple renderer.
Add a 'Restaurar' button per revision opening a confirmation dialog warning that current draft content will be overwritten, calling `pages.restoreRevision` (TASK-36) on confirm.

Using Playwright: publish two different versions of a page's content, open history, confirm both revisions are listed with correct order, preview the older one, restore it, and confirm the content editor now shows the restored content.

technicalNotes: The read-only preview should reuse `blocksToTiptapDoc` and a non-editable Tiptap render rather than building a second content renderer from scratch — the public page renderer (TASK-50) will need similar read-only rendering, so keep this reusable.
